verdict_reason: The disagreements_among_translators block flags a genuine doctrinal divergence on the work being done by yadṛcchayā: Ramanuja reads it karmically (arrival is evidence of past merit), Vishvanatha reads it operationally (no further karma-yoga needed once the dharma-yuddha is in front of you), Sridhara reads it rhetorically (why hesitate when the moment is given?), and modern translators preserve 'unsought' but lose the doctrinal weight. The explanation names the stake clearly (the militarism misreading versus the unsought-arrival reading) and identifies what each interpretive emphasis preserves. Substantive PASS.
